title: chylismia scapoidea subsp. macrocarpa
intro: Chylismia scapoidea subsp. macrocarpa, also known as the Large-flowered Bladderpod, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a member of the Onagraceae family and is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Chylismia scapoidea subsp. macrocarpa include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the roots, stems, and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
